mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'enterprise'
mission.description: [Bug]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'enterprise'. This issue affects 21+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### What happened?

Importing the `litellm.proxy` (and probably other) modules crashes with the latest PyPI release. Worked up until the latest version of the package on PyPI.

Edit: introduced inside https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/pull/10321.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'enterprise'
steps.description:
> Lite LLM guys, PLEASE DO NOT BREAK STUFF in MINOR releases. I mean, if you want to move some essential modules to the enterprise, please first mark them somehow and do the official move in 1.68. I am among the numerous people who got everything broken just because of a minor litellm update.

hi ,
```yaml
>>> import litellm.proxy
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<python-input-0>", line 1, in <module>
    import litellm.proxy
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/__init__.py", line 1058, in <module>
    from .responses.main import *
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/responses/main.py", line 13, in <module>
    from litellm.responses.litellm_completion_transformation.handler import (
        LiteLLMCompletionTransformationHandler,
    )
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/responses/litellm_completion_transformation/handler.py",
```

steps.title: Upgrade litellm to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade litellm:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade litellm litellm/litellm --namespace litellm
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm
helm list -n litellm
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
> Lite LLM guys, PLEASE DO NOT BREAK STUFF in MINOR releases. I mean, if you want to move some essential modules to the enterprise, please first mark them somehow and do the official move in 1.68. I am among the numerous people who got everything broken just because of a minor litellm update.

hi , acknowledging this feedback.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
